title: F33 podman stats with cgroupfs broken
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman run --rm -d --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs docker.io/alpine sleep 666

2. podman stats -l


**Describe the results you received:**

podman (master) $ podman stats -l
Error: unable to obtain cgroup stats: open /sys/fs/cgroup/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/user.slice/libpod_parent/libpod-d9b0700a382b6ed6b95ea0c2031dfd82c9430d696e98ab2a6d9c725323754e23.scope/memory.current: open /sys/fs/cgroup/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/user.slice/libpod_parent/libpod-d9b0700a382b6ed6b95ea0c2031dfd82c9430d696e98ab2a6d9c725323754e23.scope/memory.current: no such file or directory

comments.body:
The path is different:
* `/sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1000.slice/` exists on F33
* `/sys/fs/cgroup/user-1000.slice/` what Podman looks at
Got it. For some reason podman beliefs the container is running under systemd.

Took me a while to wrap my head around the issue.

To clarify: the upper example is failing as we're not storing the cgroup manager per container but in the engine's config. Hence, we need to use `--cgroup-manager=cgroupfs` for `podman stats` as well.

When doing that, we get the following error (which aligns with the failures from the e2e stats tests):
unable to obtain cgroup stats: open /sys/fs/cgroup/libpod_parent/libpod-c91f034f7c8cb248713db8afcb35ffab59870e2f0a0b98d0434c8d29c232cbc4/memory.current
Does not happen on cgroupsv2 (root). @giuseppe I think that's something for you
Rootless we're just not creating the paths and I don't yet know why.

> Rootless we're just not creating the paths and I don't yet know why.

rootless cannot create paths with cgroupfs.  It has not enough privileges for doing it
I have no idea how `test/e2e/stats_test.go` is then passing rootless because it's always on cgroupfs.
